data.title: Evertz Technologies Limited raises dividend by 2.5% to CAD 0.205
data.description:
• Evertz Technologies Limited (ET:CA) declares CAD 0.205/share quarterly dividend, 2.5% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.200.
  • Evertz Board of Directors also declared a special dividend of $1.00.
  • Both payable Dec. 24; for shareholders of record Dec. 18; ex-div Dec. 18.
  • See ET:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Lion Group Holding buys 88.49 Bitcoin for $8 million
data.description:
• Lion Group Holding (LGHL) on Thursday announced the acquisition of 88.49 units of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) for $8 million.
  • This move underscores LGHL's commitment to positioning Bitcoin as a core reserve asset within its portfolio.
  • LGHL remains committed to exploring strategic opportunities within the digital asset space and will provide periodic updates as it continues to grow and manage its treasury reserve.
  • Lion Group Holding (LGHL) shares are down 1.6% in morning trade, in line with the broader crypto market.

data.title: Truist, Citizens, First Horizon lose bull rates at Baird after recent climb
data.description:
Baird Equity Research on Thursday downgraded Truist Financial (TFC), Citizens Financial Group (CFG) and First Horizon (FHN) shares to Neutral from Outperform on the premise that the regional banking group's recent rally limits upside potential.

The three stocks each rose 10%-12% from a month ago, aligning with the broader State Street SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF's (KRE) 10% rise, and easily outpacing the overall S&P 500 Index's 0.5% gain.

That relative outperformance suggests "a more balanced risk/reward across the broader bank group from our perspective," analyst David George wrote in a note. 

Fundamentally, though, George noted that preprovision net revenue trends remain healthy, "credit is in good shape, and banks are getting more aggressive in terms of capital management," citing bank updates this week.

Minutes after the opening bell, TFC gained 0.6%, CFG +0.5% and FHN -0.8%.

Seeking Alpha's peer page shows various metrics comparing the three lenders.

data.title: IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum initiated with Outperform ratings at Mizuho
data.description:
IonQ (IONQ), Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) were in the spotlight on Thursday as investment firm Mizuho Securities initiated coverage on the quantum computing industry.

IonQ, Rigetti and D-Wave Quantum were all given Outperform ratings and price targets of $90, $50 and $46, respectively.

“[Quantum computing] promises a major transformation in high performance compute (HPC) following in the steps of CPUs and GPUs,” Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh wrote in a note to clients. “We note: [Quantum computing] can drive exponential [more than] 10,000x performance improvement vs. classical/AI compute; while near-term revenues could be lumpy, we see the industry beginning the steepest part of the S-curve driving revenues/earnings to 2035E and beyond; and we estimate [quantum computing total addressable market] growing from ~$1B in 2025E to ~$205B in 2035E — ~100x in 10 years. We believe [quantum computing] drives a new paradigm that offers exponential opportunities to 2035-40E as investors look for the next NVDA.”

Delving deeper, Rakesh said he sees IonQ as a leader in the quantum computing space, given that it has low error rates with trapped ions and a higher coherence time, which could drive a faster commercial ramp. It also has a full-stack solution, with computing, networking and sensing.

Rigetti could benefit as it gets to more than 150 qubits by 2026 and more than 1,000 by 2027, with a 99.8% gate fidelity rate, Rakesh said. It also has more than $450M in cash and equivalents, which suggests it may not need to raise money until at least 2030. It's also the “closest scalable superconducting [quantum computing] peer to IBM and Google,” Rakesh added.

D-Wave is on a dual track to annealing and gate model, with the potential for M&A, Rakesh said. It is also the “dominant” quantum appealing player in the near-term emerging optimization market, which accounts for roughly 20% of the total quantum computing market.

data.title: MingZhu Logistics announces Nasdaq delisting notice, shares dip
data.description:
MingZhu Logistics Holdings (YGMZ) on Thursday announced a Nasdaq delisting notice, with trading set to be suspended from December 12, 2025.

Shares were -43.59% to $0.032 during Thursday morning trading.

The delisting determination results from the company's non-compliance with the stock exchange's minimum closing bid price requirement. The rule requires the company to maintain a minimum closing bid price of $1 per share for 30 consecutive business days.

MingZhu said it requested an appeal at a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel on December 9, presenting a compliance plan that included a proposed reverse share split. However, the appeal was formally denied on December 10.

The company intends to file a further appeal within a 15-day deadline.

"While this appeal will delay the filing of Form 25 (which would finalize the delisting and deregistration with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission), it will not prevent the suspension of trading in the company’s shares on Nasdaq as scheduled on December 12," the December 11 statement by the company said.

data.title: Mingzhu Logistics receives Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• Mingzhu Logistics Holdings (YGMZ) on Thursday announced that it received a notification from Nasdaq.
  • The notification stated that the company’s securities will be delisted from the Nasdaq Capital Market, with trading suspended at the opening of trading on December 12, 2025.
  • The delisting determination stems from the company’s non-compliance with “Bid Price” rule. This rule requires listed companies to maintain a minimum closing bid price of $1 per share for 30 consecutive business days.
  • Nasdaq first notified the company of its non-compliance on October 20, 2025.
  • Typically, Nasdaq provides a 180-calendar day cure period for companies to regain compliance. 
  • YGMZ is -35.23% to $0.0364.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq open lower as Oracle post-earnings slide weighs
data.description:
The benchmark S&P 500 index and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite opened lower on Thursday after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

The S&P (SP500) fell -0.37% to 6,861.23 points, and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) dropped -0.66% to 23,497.63 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) outperformed, rising +0.34% to 48,219.07 points. 

“Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning,” stated Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid. He noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors.

Yields were lower across the curve, with the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) falling 3.5 basis points to 3.52%. The benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropped 4.2 basis points to 4.11%, while the U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) declined by 2.3 basis points to 4.76%. Short-term yields also moved lower, with the 3-month rate (US3M) decreasing by 2 basis points to 3.66%.

Top gainers after the opening bell included Nordson (NDSN) +5.58%, Mosaic (MOS) +4.46%, and Yum! Brands (YUM) +3.57%. Notable decliners were led by Oracle (ORCL) with a steep -16.42% drop, followed by Robinhood (HOOD) -4.76% and GE Vernova (GEV) -3.59%. Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase (COIN) and server manufacturer Super Micro Computer (SMCI) also weakened, falling -3.30% and -3.07%, respectively.

data.title: What analysts are saying about Coca-Cola's CEO hire
data.description:
Analysts weighed in on Thursday on Coca-Cola Company (KO) announcing that its board of directors has set a succession plan for the beverage giant, with Chief Operating Officer Henrique Braun selected to succeed longtime CEO James Quincey next year.

TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow said the firm views Braun as a high-quality successor and believes his experience in emerging markets and international operations makes him a strong fit for the CEO position. "When we met with Braun at Coca-Cola's HQ in March, we found his efforts to improve operational performance through increased transparency and more open communications with the company's bottler network as key contributors to the company's recent success,"  Moskow updated on the development.

Bank of America analyst Peter Galbo said the firm was not surprised by Braun’s selection given his increased visibility with investors over the past year. Braun was called a strong choice for Coca-Cola's (KO) bottling system, with his roots as an operator in Latin America positioning him well with the company’s most important partners as the region has experienced some macro challenges and slowdowns in recent quarters.

Galbo and his team highlighted that Braun steps into the CEO role with Coca-Cola (KO) in a much stronger position than several years ago. "KO is delivering faster organic sales growth, operating with great efficiency, and has shifted to a more asset-light model," he added. Importantly, Braun will oversee the finalizing of Coca-Cola's (KO) bottler re-franchising efforts in Africa and India and will serve at a time when KO is still litigating its transfer pricing case with the IRS.

Morgan Stanley analyst Dara Mohsenian was also generally positive about Coca-Cola's (KO) ability to replace Quincey. "Clearly it's a loss for KO, but the company is well prepared in our minds with sustained competitive advantage and is set up for the transition, and we view Braun highly," wrote Mohsenian.

Shares of Coca-Cola (KO) were up 0.8% in early trading on Thursday.

data.title: Most shorted S&P 500 stocks in November
data.description:
Charter Communications (CHTR) emerged as the most shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float in November, followed by Moderna (MRNA) and Super Micro Computer (SMCI).

Charter Communications (CHTR) had 19.05% of its shares sold short as of November 28, up from 17.62% at the end of October. The stock carries a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant model, with weak scores in growth and momentum, and its one-year share price is down nearly 44%. The company's high debt levels, lack of a dividend, and limited near-term growth catalysts present challenges, with management's focus on future promises not yet delivering immediate results, explained Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian.

Moderna (MRNA) had 19.01% of its shares sold short as of November 28, up from 17.62% at October-end. The biotechnology company's shares have fallen 32% over the past year, largely underperforming the broader market and health care sector fund (XLV), which has advanced 7% in the same period. The company’s recent Q3 report showed a continued decline in COVID vaccine sales. The company's dependence on COVID vaccine sales, amidst dwindling demand and competition, challenges near-term revenue growth and market performance, argued Seeking Alpha analyst Stephen Ayers.

Super Micro Computer (SMCI) took the third spot, with 16.86% of its shares sold short as of November, up from 14.98% in the prior month. Shares have fallen 10% over the past month and 8% over the past year. Super Micro Computer faces ongoing margin pressures, with its gross margin declining to 9.5% and expectations of further contraction, raising concerns about long-term profitability in a competitive AI server market, explained Envision Research.
